questions.question: Which structure is found in the cortex of lymph node?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Lymphatic nodules

questions.question: The paracortex of lymph node is rich in:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. T lymphocytes

questions.question: Afferent lymphatic vessels enter the lymph node at:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Capsule

questions.question: Thymus dependent zone of lymph node is present in:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Paracortex

questions.question: White pulp of the spleen surrounds:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Central artery

questions.question: Red pulp of the spleen contains:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Splenic cords and sinusoids

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the spleen?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Destruction of old RBCs

questions.question: The periarterial lymphatic sheath (PALS) contains mainly:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. T lymphocytes

questions.question: Thymus dependent zone of spleen is present in:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. White pulp

questions.question: Thymus is involved in the maturation of:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. T lymphocytes

questions.question: Which feature is a characteristic of the thymus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Hassall's corpuscles

questions.question: The thymus lacks:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Afferent lymphatic vessels

questions.question: The cortex of the thymus appears darker due to:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Dense T lymphocytes

questions.question: Tonsillar crypts are:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Deep epithelial invaginations

questions.question: Palatine tonsils are covered by:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Stratified squamous epithelium

questions.question: Which tonsil is covered by pseudostratified columnar ciliated epithelium?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Pharyngeal tonsil

questions.question: Tonsils are considered one of:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Secondary lymphoid organs

questions.question: Thymus is one of:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Primary lymphoid organs

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why the thymic cortex appears darker than the medulla?
options: Higher density of macrophages and plasma cells
Larger number of immune-incompetent T-lymphocytes and epithelial reticular cells
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Larger number of immune-incompetent T-lymphocytes and epithelial reticular cells

questions.question: Unlike other lymphoid organs, the thymus lacks:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Reticular fibers

questions.question: Hassall's corpuscles are found exclusively in which part of the thymus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Medulla

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the blood-thymus barrier?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Afferent lymphatic vessels

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the thymus after puberty?
options: It becomes infiltrated by adipose tissue and involutes
It increases production of Hassall's corpuscles
It becomes a secondary lymphoid organ
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. It becomes infiltrated by adipose tissue and involutes

questions.question: The primary function of the blood-thymus barrier is to:
options: Prevent T-lymphocytes from leaving the thymus
Isolate immature T-lymphocytes from blood-borne antigens
Facilitate entry of plasma cells into the cortex
Allow direct mixing of blood and lymph in the thymus
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Isolate immature T-lymphocytes from blood-borne antigens

questions.question: Which of the following is absent in the thymus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Lymphatic nodules

questions.question: The reticular network of the thymus is formed by:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Processes of epithelial reticular cells

questions.question: T-lymphocytes (thymocytes) in the thymus cortex are best described as:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Immune-incompetent and proliferating

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature unique to the thymus among lymphoid organs?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Presence of Hassall's corpuscles

questions.question: The thymus is classified as a:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Primary (central) lymphoid organ

questions.question: What happens to Hassall's corpuscles with advancing age?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Their number increases

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes epithelial reticular cells?
options: They have dark cytoplasm with many lysosomes
Their processes join by desmosomes and occluding junctions
They are derived from circulating monocytes
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Their processes join by desmosomes and occluding junctions

questions.question: Which cell type is notably absent in the normal thymus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Plasma cells
